ELPAC Level 4
NWEA 300–650L
DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS10-HSVPA910-SRW-IMR-01 • HS Visual and Performing Arts: Theatre 9-10 Grade 10 • Character Relationships in Drama (Pr4a)

The Last Visa Letter

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What does the girl ask the boy to do?
2QUESTION 2: Why does the boy say the audience won't feel anything if the actors don't look at each other?
3QUESTION 3: Why does the girl suggest the mother holds onto her end of the letter for a second?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Write three things: what the girl asks to do at the start, what the boy says the look has to carry, and what the girl says the held letter will let the audience feel.
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5  |  CCSS.ELA-Literacy.SL.9-10.3  |  HS Visual and Performing Arts: Theatre 9-10: Character Relationships (Pr4a)
